title: Go WebP encoder dependency decision

current_python_thumbnail_contract.owner: Python upload/import thumbnail generation through optional Pillow
current_python_thumbnail_contract.thumbnail_only_success_behavior: returns the thumbnail URL and deletes/does not retain the original upload only after thumbnail generation succeeds
current_python_thumbnail_contract.cleanup_contract: cleanup and note image cleanup must continue to discover _thumb.webp companion files

candidate_evaluation.reason: Package exposes Decode and DecodeConfig but no WebP encoder, so it cannot satisfy the 23.8-thumb.1 encode-support gate.
It can encode WebP, but its cgo/GCC/MinGW toolchain requirement adds Windows and Pi packaging risk for this track.
It provides libwebp-backed lossy encoding controls, but requires libwebp system packages and cgo, which conflicts with the small packaging surface desired for Windows/local and Pi arm64.
It is pure Go and encodes WebP, but encodes VP8L lossless output; Prism currently uses quality=80 lossy WebP thumbnails, so it is not the first parity candidate.
Pure Go encoder with lossy/lossless options, quality 0-100 controls, alpha support, and no cgo dependency; best matches Prism's current quality=80 WebP thumbnail contract for a future spike.

selected_candidate.selection_scope: first spike candidate only, not a production dependency in this gate
required_future_validation: Python-vs-Go thumbnail parity fixtures for jpeg/png/webp/gif inputs
thumbnail_only success and failure behavior fixtures
max width 500px and quality 80 output behavior fixtures
explicit retained-Python fallback for unsupported or failed Go thumbnail generation until Pillow removal is separately approved
packaging smoke after adding the dependency to the real go-shadow module

build_probe.operation: decode with Go standard library image decoders, encode WebP with Lossy=true Quality=80, then decode output with github.com/skrashevich/go-webp/decoder
limits: Probe used synthetic images only; production upload/import parity fixtures are still required.
Probe did not add the module to go-shadow/go.mod.
Probe did not run on the Raspberry Pi device; it verified linux/arm64 cross-build with CGO_ENABLED=0.

not_authorized_by_23_8_thumb_1: Implement Go upload or thumbnail routes
Remove Python PIL imports or fallback behavior
Write production static/uploads or docs/attachments files
Expose Prism directly to public internet

fallback_policy.future_go_candidate: Go thumbnail generation must remain behind explicit local/copied-data or disabled-by-default gates until parity is proven.
fallback_policy.unsupported_or_failed_go_case: Retain Python fallback unless a later Pillow removal gate explicitly proves and documents safe removal.

allowed_next_step.title: Thumbnail parity fixtures
allowed_next_step.summary: Lock upload, upload-url, import helper, jpg/png/webp/gif handling, thumbnail_only, _thumb.webp paths, max width 500px, quality 80, failure no-mutation behavior, and cleanup companion-file discovery before any Go implementation or Pillow removal.
